source_jobs.title: General Interest
source_jobs.description:
About Incandescent

Incandescent is a strategy consulting and venture development firm; we advise leaders, conduct research, and work with founders to build companies. We serve CEOs of Fortune 500 companies across a range of industries (similar to a traditional management consulting firm), founders of early- and growth-stage ventures (often compensated with equity in our client’s venture), and visionary leaders in social sector organizations (such as The Rockefeller Foundation and Acumen). Across these different types of engagements, our focus is often on collaborating with leaders to drive solutions at the intersection of strategy and organization. Founded in 2013, we’ve been building a small team, slowly and carefully. Each team member plays an integral part in developing Incandescent as a firm that discovers better ways to create, build and run organizations. 

 

We build lasting relationships—with our clients and with each other. Our team members contribute to meaningful work while growing through deliberate practice and collaborative learning. We're always interested in connecting with exceptional individuals who share our commitments and vision. If you're drawn to our approach and values, we welcome your resume below and will reach out should an opportunity align with your background and aspirations.

source_jobs.title: Special Assistant
source_jobs.description:
About Incandescent



Incandescent is an advisory firm discovering how to create, build, and run enterprises to do the world’s most important work. We focus our work on four primary platforms:

 - Corporates & Fortune 500s https://www.incandescent.com/work/advise, including a dedicated focus on Private Equity. We work with leaders with ambitious visions, whose aspirations reach beyond simply optimizing their current operations or applying known best practices.

 - Systems Change Organizations https://www.incandescent.com/work/social-impact, including philanthropies, non-profit organizations, and mission-driven enterprises.

 - Ventures https://www.incandescent.com/work/ventures, from late seed stage through Series B. We act as partners to founders, growing ideas into businesses that scale and endure.

Incandescent was founded in 2013, and we’ve been building a small team, slowly and carefully, since then. In January 2026, we acquired Minds at Work founded by Bob Kegan & Lisa Lahey, a firm with a distinctive body of work in Adult Development, Immunity to Change, and helping leaders and organizations become Deliberately Developmental Organizations. You can read more about the acquisition here https://www.onhumanenterprise.com/frogs-butterflies-announcing-incandescents-acquisition-of-minds-at-work.



Essence of the Role

We’re looking for a Special Assistant who can bring leverage and life to two of our priorities for Minds at Work: supporting the firm’s co-founder, Bob Kegan, in his day-to-day work, and driving the operational work behind Minds at Work’s Public Programs so they run with precision and care. Bob is the author of Immunity to Change and An Everyone Culture, and a celebrated teacher and advisor whose work has influenced leaders and organizations around the world. He travels globally and coaches senior executives across sectors. The role is closely connected to the ideas, programs, and relationships surrounding Adult Development, Immunity to Change, and the wider Minds at Work body of work. The role is demanding and fast-paced, with significant responsibility from the outset that demands a proactive, entrepreneurial mindset & a strong learning and development orientation.

The two areas of responsibility are different in important ways, but they call on the same core strengths: organization, sound judgment, anticipation, responsiveness, and a talent for turning recurring work into reliable, repeatable systems. The right person will help shape and grow the role over time.



Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:

 - Supporting the co-founder. You will manage the co-founder’s calendar with precision, help him stay ahead of his priorities, and prepare the materials, briefings, and background he needs for meetings, travel, and recurring commitments. You will communicate regularly with colleagues, external partners, and clients, always with the discretion and care that working closely with a co-founder requires. As you grow into the role, you will help build the systems that keep his working life organized and easy to navigate.

 - Producing Public Programs. You will own the operational work that keeps Minds at Work’s Public Programs running smoothly before, during, and after each session: participant communications and tracking, registration coordination, faculty preparation and briefings, management of the run of show, and live support during sessions.

 - Building better systems and AI-enabled workflows. Across both areas, you will help create the trackers, documentation, routines, and AI-enabled workflows that make recurring work dependable and straightforward for others to pick up. You will notice where work can be organized more simply, made less reliant on any one person, or improved through thoughtful use of AI, and partner with the team to steadily strengthen how the work gets done.

 - Contributing across the firm. Every member of our team, regardless of title, plays an important role in building Incand

source_jobs.title: Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Incandescent



Incandescent is an advisory firm discovering how to create, build, and run enterprises to do the world’s most important work. We focus our work on three primary platforms:



 - Corporates & Fortune 500s https://www.incandescent.com/work/advise, including a dedicated focus on Private Equity. We work with leaders with ambitious visions, whose aspirations reach beyond simply optimizing their current operations or applying known best practices. Our work focuses on:
   
   - Strategy: shaping and redefining strategic direction at the enterprise level
   
   - Execution: building the organization, culture, and talent necessary to achieve a new vision, a new level of performance, or a new magnitude of scale
   
   - Innovation: creating new businesses and new capabilities within established organizations

 - Systems Change Organizations https://www.incandescent.com/work/social-impact, including philanthropies, non-profit organizations and mission-driven enterprises. In this platform, we focus on:
   
   - Shaping strategy for systems change actors
   
   - Partnering with for-profit and non-profit entrepreneurs as they create and scale new organizations
   
   - Applying practices in organization, culture and talent to social sector organizations
   
   - Building partnerships among organizations that see the world differently – across sectors, disciplines, ideological lines and organizational stages

 - Ventures https://www.incandescent.com/work/ventures, from late seed stage through Series B. We act as partners to founders, growing ideas into businesses that scale and endure, through two main ways:
   
   - Acting as a co-founder to launch and build a venture
   
   - Playing a long-term “right-hand” advisor role to founders and their leadership teams



Incandescent was founded in 2013 and we’ve been building a small team, slowly and carefully, since then. To accelerate our capabilities, we recently acquired Minds at Work, which we encourage you to read more about here https://www.onhumanenterprise.com/frogs-butterflies-announcing-incandescents-acquisition-of-minds-at-work.



Essence of the Role



We're looking for an Associate who can “hit the ground running”. This team member would focus primarily on our corporate advisory practice, while also having the opportunity to contribute to our work in systems change and venture building. The breadth of our work and our small team structure make this role demanding and fast-paced, with a strong learning and development orientation and significant client exposure.



The Associate serves as an advisor and consultant, working in small teams (generally 2-3 people) and working directly for senior client stakeholders. The small team size gives each team member significant ownership and client-facing responsibility from the beginning. As generalists, we serve clients on a broad range of issues related to strategy execution: advising senior leaders on how organization, culture, and talent can be used as levers for driving change and accelerating growth.



Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:



 - Conduct primary and secondary research on client organizations, industries, and business problems, turning ambiguous questions into useful inputs, hypotheses, and working points of view for the team.



 - Synthesize what we are learning across interviews, meetings, and background materials, capturing high-quality notes to distill key insights, decisions, tensions, and next steps to move the work forward with clarity.



 - Support and lead meetings with clients and other stakeholders, using strong listening, sound judgment, and empathy to gather insight, test hypotheses, and build trust-based working relationships.



 - Prepare polished, client-ready materials, including decks, memos, and other working documents, translating complex ideas into clear, compelling communication for senior audiences and helping advance the team’s thinking and recommendations.



Ever

notes: No tech roles posted; appears to be a strategy consulting and venture development firm
